{{Quote|''Heavy Artillery Guns''… Unless those walls are made from ''Mandalorian iron'', this battle isn't going to last long!|[[Ahsoka Tano/Legends|Ahsoka Tano]], to [[R7-A7/Legends|R7-A7]]|<ref name="The Feral Queen" />}}
The '''Heavy Artillery Gun (HAG)''' was a mobile [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] [[Artillery/Legends|artillery]] piece used by the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] and superficially resembled their [[Multi-Troop Transport/Legends|Multi-Troop Transports]].

==Description==
The HAG greatly resembled the MTT, but replaced the troop carrier bay with a massive artillery cannon, which had tremendous range. It had heavy [[Armor/Legends|armor]] which made it very resistant to laser cannons though [[Bomb/Legends|bombs]] proved effective at destroying them.<ref name=":0" />

It had very poor speed and maneuverability, and needed to anchor itself in place with two stabilizer paddles in order to fire its main cannon.

==History==
The HAG saw its first major engagement during the Trade Federation's [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]]<ref name=":0" /> in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]<ref name="The New Essential Chronology">[[Star Wars: Starfighter (video game)|''Star Wars: Starfighter'']] takes place during the [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|Invasion of Naboo]]. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' establishes that the invasion happens in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]].</ref> while [[Battle of the Naboo resistance base|attacking a]] [[Naboo resistance base]]. Though the HAGs held the potential to devastate the turrets protecting the encampment, their heavy armor did not protect them from the powerful explosives of [[Havoc (starfighter)|''Havoc'']], [[Nym/Legends|Nym's]] personal craft, as he helped defend the encampment.<ref name=":0" />

Later, they took part in [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|the battle with the Gungan Grand army]].<ref name=":0" />

During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the HAGs were also used by the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]].<ref name="The Feral Queen" /> In [[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]],<ref name="Timeline">"[[The Feral Queen]]" is set around the time of "[[Heroes on Both Sides]]" ([[21 BBY/Legends|21 BBY]]), which marks the debut of revamped appearances for several main characters. Considering that the [[Star Wars comic books|comics]] featured in [[Star Wars Comic|''Star Wars Comic UK'']] are published in chronological order, and that "''[[Burn the Behemoth!]]''" is the first comic in the series to feature the new character models, it can be concluded that "''The Feral Queen''" takes place in 21 BBY.</ref> they were used in the [[Siege of Felacat|siege]] of [[Felacat]], encroaching on the [[Royal Felacatian Palace]]. The [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Ahsoka Tano/Legends|Ahsoka Tano]] was sent to secure [[Monarchy/Legends|Quene]] [[Miaria Prrt]] after a month of struggle. The Queen rebuffed her, and used her polymorphic abilities to change and charge the droid army. She inspired her people to do the same, and the battle ended with the droid forces torn apart.<ref name="The Feral Queen" />

==Behind the scenes==
The HAG first appeared in the [[2001]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: Starfighter (video game)|''Star Wars: Starfighter'']].<ref name=":0" />
